normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance vector has length n (one entry per vertex).
Each entry is an optional distance: Some d if a path exists, otherwise None.
The distance from the source to itself is 0.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem none_implies_no_path (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v] = none → ¬ (∃ p : Path g s v, p.lengthCost w < ∞)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns None for a vertex, then there is no path from the source to that vertex.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem some_implies_path (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v] = some d → ∃ p : Path g s v, p.lengthCost w = d
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some d for a vertex, then there exists a path from the source to that vertex whose total weight is exactly d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem upper_bound (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n, ∀ p : Path g s v,
    dist[v].elim (fun _ => True) (fun d => d ≤ p.lengthCost w)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: For any reachable vertex v and any path p from s to v, the computed distance is ≤ the cost of that path (so it is an upper bound on the shortest path).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem optimality (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v].elim (fun _ => True)
      (fun d => ∀ d' : Nat,
        (∃ p : Path g s v, p.lengthCost w = d') → d ≤ d' ∧ (d' = d → True))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Optimality: when a distance is returned as Some d, it is minimal among all path costs to v.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem relaxation_invariant (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ e : g.Edge,
    let u := e.src; let v := e.dst;
    dist[⟨u.1, _⟩].elim (fun _ => True)
      (fun du =>
        dist[⟨v.1, _⟩].elim (fun _ => True)
          (fun dv => dv ≤ du + w e))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Triangle inequality / relaxation property: for every edge u→v, if distances are defined then dv ≤ du + weight(u,v).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem nonneg_weights_required (w : g.Edge → Nat) :
  ∀ e : g.Edge, 0 ≤ w e
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Edge weights are non-negative (Nat), which is the key precondition for Dijkstra correctness.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem algorithm_terminates (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) :
  ∃ t : Nat, dijkstra_ref_steps g w s n = t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The reference implementation terminates (e.g., due to finite vertex set and bounded priority-queue operations).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem correctness_matches_spec (g : DirectedGraph) (w : g.Edge → Nat) (s : g.V) (n : Nat) (hV : g.Vcard = n) :
  let dist := dijkstra_ref g w s n;
  ∀ v : Fin n,
    dist[v] = shortestPathCostOption g w s v
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The computed vector matches the mathematical shortest-path cost function (Some shortest cost, or None if unreachable).
